meta.title: PTSD Symptom Reflection
meta.description: A private 500-statement reflection across 25 trauma-related symptom and functioning dimensions.

levels.label: Very High Current Symptom Burden
levels.desc: Your responses suggest severe and wide-ranging trauma-related symptoms. A prompt professional assessment is strongly encouraged.

levels.label: High Current Symptom Burden
levels.desc: Your responses suggest substantial trauma-related symptoms across several areas. Consider arranging an assessment with a trauma-informed professional.

levels.label: Moderate Current Symptom Burden
levels.desc: You report a meaningful mixture of trauma-related difficulties and areas of stability. Professional support may help clarify your needs.

levels.label: Mild Current Symptom Burden
levels.desc: Some trauma-related reactions appear present, though their intensity or reach may be limited. Monitor distress and daily-life impact.

levels.label: Few Current Symptoms Endorsed
levels.desc: You endorsed relatively few current trauma-related symptoms. This result cannot rule out PTSD or other concerns.

questions.text: During the past several weeks, unwanted memories of a distressing event enter my mind.
In everyday life, distressing-event memories rarely enter my mind unexpectedly.
When something reminds me of the event, trauma-related images or thoughts interrupt what I am doing.
In situations that are objectively safe, I can usually choose when to think about what happened.
